scope: FE-originated requests to the proxy from su-search-dev
audit_method: static analysis of public/integration.js, public/search-page-autocomplete.js, public/js/search-index.js, public/js/modules/*.js, pages/api/*.ts, plus one-level dependency reads of lib/api-client.ts, lib/utils.ts, lib/ip-service.ts, components/SearchInput.tsx, components/ResultList.tsx
stop_rules_applied: Cache logic in lib/cache.ts not traversed; cache_gate field records presence/absence only.
Sibling repo funnelback-proxy-dev not read; proxy_route values cross-reference the proxy dictionary by name.
lib/ files read one level deep; their imports (e.g., axios) not followed.

search-bundle.js not enumerated as a live request surface — preloaded but never executed per CLAUDE.md; its proxy-call patterns mirror integration.js and search-page-autocomplete.js (captured as cross-cutting observation).

fe_call_layers: Server-side: Next.js API routes (pages/api/*.ts) call the proxy through lib/api-client.ts (axios). BACKEND_API_URL = process.env.BACKEND_API_URL || 'https://funnelback-proxy-dev.vercel.app/proxy'.

Client-side direct: browser modules call the proxy directly via fetch / sendBeacon, using a hardcoded proxyBaseUrl 'https://funnelback-proxy-dev.vercel.app/proxy' (with a window.seattleUConfig.search.proxyBaseUrl override path in some places).

configuration_layers_observed.integration_js_defaults: public/integration.js defines const config = { apiBaseUrl, proxyBaseUrl, collection: 'seattleu~sp-search', profile: '_default', minQueryLength: 3, debounceTime: 200, prefetchDebounceTime: 300, prefetchMinQueryLength: 4, cacheTTL: 300, logLevel }. Spread into window.seattleUConfig.search via { ...config, ...window.seattleUConfig?.search } so a pre-existing window.seattleUConfig.search wins on key collision.
configuration_layers_observed.search_index_js_config: public/js/search-index.js passes { proxyBaseUrl: 'https://funnelback-proxy-dev.vercel.app/proxy', enabledModules: [...], defaultResultsPerPage, searchInputSelector, resultsContainerSelector } to searchManager.init(); these are merged via { ...this.config, ...options } in core-search-manager.js — runtime options win.
configuration_layers_observed.core_search_manager_defaults: public/js/modules/core-search-manager.js holds its own hardcoded proxyBaseUrl, observerConfig, analyticsEndpoints object, plus enableIpTracking, ipMismatchThreshold. No collection/profile/form fields — those live only in the integration.js config and per-call construction.
configuration_layers_observed.data_star_attributes_on_input: No code in the inspected files reads data-* attributes off the search input element. The input IDs referenced are 'search-input' (header) and 'autocomplete-concierge-inputField' (results page). No getAttribute('data-collection') / data-profile / data-form found in any FE module.
configuration_layers_observed.per_call_construction: Each FE call site constructs its trinity by either (a) reading from window.seattleUConfig.search / config object, or (b) forwarding the query string of a Funnelback-generated href verbatim. No call site reads the input element to source trinity values.

request_types.user_action: Land on /search-test/?query=<q> (typically via header form redirect from any seattleu.edu page)

collection.resolution_notes: Read at performSearch via config.collection; could be overridden by a pre-existing window.seattleUConfig.search.collection at script-load time, but no such override is set anywhere in this repo.

form.resolution_notes: Literal string 'partial' embedded at integration.js:906 inside the URLSearchParams construction; not configurable.

other_query_params.name: query
other_query_params.value_or_source: URL parameter 'query' from window.location, then normalizeQuery() (lowercase, trim, collapse spaces, strip ['"?!.,])
other_query_params.notes: Normalization is applied client-side before the request is fired.

other_query_params.name: sessionId
other_query_params.value_or_source: window.SessionService.getSessionId() if available; appended only when truthy

headers_set_by_fe.browser_request_to_/api/search: default fetch() headers only — no custom headers set on this call from integration.js performSearch.

request_types.cache_gate: Indirect: processUrlParameters first calls window.checkForPreRenderedContent (defined by search-page-autocomplete.js) which itself fires a separate /api/search request with X-Cache-Status check (see request type 'pre-render-content-check'). If that returns content, performSearch is skipped. If it returns nothing or fails, performSearch fires.

request_types.user_action: Same trigger as results-page-initial-render; this fires first as a check for cached pre-rendered content.
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → su-search-dev /api/search (server reads cache; on HIT returns cached HTML, on MISS hits proxy → /funnelback/search)
request_types.cache_gate: This request itself IS the cache probe. Server-side /api/search consults lib/cache.ts; FE reads response.headers['X-Cache-Status'] to log HIT/MISS but always uses the response body either way.
gaps: When the call falls through to the proxy on cache MISS, the FE-side request is indistinguishable on the wire from a normal performSearch call (same trinity, same form=partial). This auditable path produces a duplicate proxy hit with the integration.js-side performSearch if both fire (mitigated by the pre-render success-then-return early-exit in processUrlParameters).

request_types.user_action: User submits the global header search form on any seattleu.edu page
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → POST su-search-dev /api/pre-render → proxy /funnelback/search (fire-and-forget background fetch from server)

collection.source_layer: pre-render.ts handler default; FE does not send collection in the POST body
collection.resolution_notes: pre-render.ts:112 defaults collection = 'seattleu~sp-search' when req.body.collection is absent. integration.js POSTs only { query, sessionId }.

profile.source_layer: pre-render.ts handler default; FE does not send profile in the POST body

other_query_params.name: sessionId
other_query_params.value_or_source: POST body 'sessionId'; from window.SessionService.getSessionId(); pre-render.ts substitutes 'prerender_<ts>_<rand>' if absent

other_query_params.name: clientIp
other_query_params.value_or_source: Server-resolved via getClientInfo(req.headers); appended to backend params if present

triggering_code.function: handler — composes searchParams and fires apiClient.get('/funnelback/search', ...)

request_types.cache_gate: Server-side: pre-render.ts caches the response with 16h TTL via setCachedSearchResults (lib/cache.ts) — not traversed.
gaps: FE never sends collection/profile in the POST body; the server-side defaults are the de facto trinity. If any caller ever wants to pre-render against a non-default collection/profile, no FE call site supplies that today.

request_types.user_action: User types in the global header search input (>= 3 chars after debounceTime=200ms)
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → su-search-dev /api/suggestions → proxy /funnelback/suggest + /suggestPeople + /suggestPrograms (3 parallel server-side fetches when type query param is absent)

collection.source_layer: hardcoded in suggestions.ts fetchGeneralSuggestions params (only applies to /funnelback/suggest leg)
collection.resolution_notes: FE does not send collection. /suggestPeople and /suggestPrograms legs do not take a collection at all.

profile.source_layer: hardcoded in suggestions.ts fetchGeneralSuggestions params (only /funnelback/suggest leg)

form.resolution_notes: Suggestions endpoints do not take a form parameter.

other_query_params.name: query
other_query_params.value_or_source: FE sends raw input value as 'query'; suggestions.ts forwards as 'partial_query' to /funnelback/suggest, and as 'query' to /suggestPeople and /suggestPrograms
other_query_params.notes: Parameter name renamed at the FE-server boundary for the general leg.

other_query_params.name: sessionId
other_query_params.value_or_source: FE sends from SessionService; suggestions.ts forwards on each leg

other_query_params.name: type
other_query_params.value_or_source: Not sent by FE in this code path (defaults to all-types fan-out)
other_query_params.notes: suggestions.ts supports type=general|staff|programs but no FE caller exercises this.

headers_set_by_fe.browser_request_to_/api/suggestions: default fetch() headers only — no custom headers from integration.js fetchHeaderSuggestions.

request_types.cache_gate: Server-side: suggestions.ts checks lib/cache.ts via getCachedData/setCachedData with key 'suggestions:<type>:<query>:<collection>'. FE does not pre-check cache for this request type.
gaps: FE never sets the type query parameter. The type=general/staff/programs branches in suggestions.ts:79-91 are dead from this repo's call sites — only the default fan-out leg is reachable.

request_types.user_action: User types in the search-results-page input #autocomplete-concierge-inputField (>= 3 chars after 200ms debounce). Note this script attaches site-wide despite the file name, but the input element it targets exists only on /search-test/.
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → su-search-dev /api/suggestions → fan-out as in header-search-suggestions-keystroke

collection.source_layer: hardcoded in suggestions.ts fetchGeneralSuggestions (FE does not send)
collection.resolution_notes: Same as header keystroke — server-side hardcode is the de facto value.

other_query_params.name: query
other_query_params.notes: Differs from integration.js code path which normalizes.

request_types.cache_gate: Server-side cache only (same path as header-search-suggestions-keystroke).

request_types.originating_component: public/integration.js — prefetchSearchResults (debounced; bound to both header input AND results-page input)
request_types.user_action: User types in either the header search input or the results-page search input, query length >= 4 (config.prefetchMinQueryLength), debounce 300ms (config.prefetchDebounceTime).
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → su-search-dev /api/prefetch → proxy /funnelback/search (server fires fire-and-forget; client does not block)

collection.resolution_notes: Sent on the wire via URLSearchParams in prefetchSearchResults at integration.js:486.

form.source_layer: not sent by FE; not added by prefetch.ts to backend params
form.resolution_notes: prefetch.ts does NOT add form: 'partial' on the backend leg — distinct from pre-render.ts and search.ts.

other_query_params.name: prefetch
other_query_params.notes: Set in BOTH the FE-→-/api/prefetch URL params AND propagated by prefetch.ts on the backend leg.

other_query_params.name: sessionId
other_query_params.value_or_source: SessionService.getSessionId() if available; appended to FE URL

other_query_params.name: ttl
other_query_params.value_or_source: Not sent by FE; prefetch.ts defaults to 300 seconds when absent

other_query_params.name: clientIp
other_query_params.value_or_source: Server-side getClientInfo() result, propagated to backend params by prefetch.ts

headers_set_by_fe.fetch_options: priority: low, keepalive: true, AbortController with 5000ms timeout

request_types.cache_gate: Server-side caches under generateSearchCacheKey(query, collection, profile) with cacheTTL (lib/cache.ts). Not FE-side.
gaps: Trinity for prefetch lacks form on the backend leg, while results-page-initial-render sends form=partial. If the proxy treats /funnelback/search differently when form is absent vs 'partial', the prefetched payload may not match what a subsequent results-page-initial-render call expects from the cache key. Worth flagging in cross-cutting (FE-cache-key consistency).

request_types.user_action: On the results page, user re-submits the search form (modifies query and clicks search).

collection.resolution_notes: Same as results-page-initial-render — they share the private performSearch function.

request_types.cache_gate: Server-side cache only — same path as results-page-initial-render.

request_types.originating_component: public/search-page-autocomplete.js — performSearch (local fn, distinct from integration.js's window.performSearch)
request_types.user_action: User clicks a suggestion in the search-page autocomplete dropdown (general/staff/program). Triggers performSearch + updateUrl.

form.source_layer: FE does NOT send form; /api/search server-side defaults form = req.query.form || 'partial' at search.ts:249
form.resolution_notes: DIVERGENCE from integration.js performSearch which sends form=partial explicitly. End wire trinity is identical only because /api/search defaults the missing param.

other_query_params.name: query
other_query_params.value_or_source: Suggestion text (no normalization applied client-side here)

triggering_code.function: performSearch (private, file-local — not the same as window.performSearch)

gaps: Two distinct performSearch implementations exist with different parameter shapes (form sent vs not sent, normalization applied vs not). Cross-cutting note flags this.

request_types.originating_component: public/js/modules/tabs-manager.js — loadTabContent (and enhancedPerformSearch when re-entering via window.performSearch wrap)
request_types.user_action: User clicks a tab in the search results page (.tab-list__nav a, .tab__button, [role=tab], [data-tab-group-control], etc.).
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → proxy /funnelback/search DIRECTLY (no Next.js hop). Goes through core.fetchFromProxy(href, 'search').

collection.value: varies — taken from the Funnelback-generated tab anchor href query string
collection.source_layer: Funnelback response HTML embedded the trinity into the tab href; FE forwards as-is
collection.resolution_notes: fetchFromProxy parses href.split('?')[1] into URLSearchParams, removes only sessionId/clientIp, then re-injects them. Whatever collection/profile/form Funnelback put in the href is what reaches the proxy.

profile.value: varies — typically '_default' or a tab-specific profile per Funnelback's URL templating

form.resolution_notes: lib/utils.ts:isTabRequest treats 'form=partial' as a strong tab indicator.

other_query_params.name: f.Tabs|<group>
other_query_params.notes: lib/utils.ts:extractTabId parses Faculty_Staff / Programs / News / Results from this and from staff= / programs- / Faculty patterns.

other_query_params.name: start_rank, num_ranks, query, etc.
other_query_params.value_or_source: Whatever Funnelback embedded in the href

other_query_params.name: sessionId
other_query_params.notes: FE strips any incoming sessionId and re-injects its canonical one.

other_query_params.name: clientIp
other_query_params.notes: FE strips incoming clientIp and re-injects its canonical one.

headers_set_by_fe.browser_request_to_proxy: default fetch() headers only — fetchFromProxy does not add custom headers.

request_types.cache_gate: None FE-side. (Direct proxy fetch — neither lib/cache.ts nor /api/search interpose.)
gaps: CONTRADICTS the audit's working assumption. Tab clicks DO fire a proxy request. The /search-test/ initial render does not pre-load all tabs' worth of data; each tab activation produces its own /proxy/funnelback/search call.
Each tab click bypasses the /api/search server-side cache layer entirely — nothing in this code path consults lib/cache.ts. The proxy's own caching (if any) is the only mediator.

request_types.user_action: User clicks a facet link in .facet-group__list (excluding clear and show-more).

other_query_params.name: f.<FacetName>|<value>
other_query_params.notes: Includes f.Tabs, f.Authors, f.Topics, etc., depending on facet category.

request_types.user_action: User clicks a.facet-group__clear or .facet-breadcrumb__link to remove an active facet.

other_query_params.name: Funnelback's clear-facet href
other_query_params.notes: Differs from facet-select href in that the f.<facet> param it removed is absent.

request_types.user_action: User clicks an a.pagination__link (numbered page, next, or previous).

other_query_params.name: start_rank
other_query_params.notes: Also extracted by FE for client-side URL update via updateUrlWithoutRefresh.

other_query_params.name: page
other_query_params.notes: May or may not be present depending on Funnelback's pagination scheme.

request_types.user_action: User clicks a 'Did you mean' suggestion (.search-spelling-suggestions__link) or a query-blending highlight (.query-blending__highlight).

collection.resolution_notes: fetchFromProxy 'spelling' branch parses href query string, strips sessionId/clientIp, re-injects them, forwards as-is.

form.resolution_notes: Spelling endpoints typically return JSON; form may or may not appear.

request_types.originating_component: Three independent emitters: public/integration.js trackResultClick, public/search-page-autocomplete.js trackResultClick, public/js/modules/analytics-manager.js trackResultClick → core.sendAnalyticsData (type='click').
request_types.user_action: User clicks a result link (.fb-result h3 a, .search-result-item h3 a, .listing-item__title a).
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → proxy /analytics/click DIRECTLY via navigator.sendBeacon (preferred) or fetch(keepalive)

request_body_fields.name: originalQuery
request_body_fields.value_or_source: core.originalQuery || URL ?query || searchInput.value || '' (analytics-manager); query from URL params (integration.js); query function arg (search-page-autocomplete.js)
request_body_fields.notes: Three different recovery orders across emitters.

request_body_fields.name: clickedUrl
request_body_fields.notes: data-live-url preferred for accurate destination tracking.

request_body_fields.name: timestamp
request_body_fields.value_or_source: Date.now() or new Date().toISOString() depending on emitter

request_body_fields.name: sessionId
request_body_fields.value_or_source: SessionService / SessionManager / core.getSessionId() — only set when truthy

request_body_fields.name: clientIp
request_body_fields.value_or_source: core.getClientIp() — only added by analytics-manager-via-core path
request_body_fields.notes: Other emitters do not include clientIp.

headers_set_by_fe.fetch_options: keepalive: true (when navigator.sendBeacon falls back to fetch)

gaps: Three independent emitters of the same event — payload shape varies (timestamp format, clientIp inclusion, originalQuery recovery). On any given click, multiple may fire if their event listeners overlap (analytics-manager and search-page-autocomplete.js both attach to .fb-result h3 a). No deduplication observed.

request_types.originating_component: Two emitters: public/integration.js window.trackSuggestionClick (header suggestions) and public/search-page-autocomplete.js trackSuggestionClick (search-page suggestions).
request_types.user_action: User clicks a suggestion item (general, staff, or program) in either header or search-page autocomplete.

request_body_fields.name: originalQuery
request_body_fields.notes: Note this puts the SUGGESTION text into 'originalQuery' rather than the user's original typed query — distinct semantics from result-click events.

request_body_fields.name: clickedUrl
request_body_fields.value_or_source: url arg, or '' for general suggestions

request_body_fields.name: clickedTitle
request_body_fields.value_or_source: title arg with role/dept context appended for staff, school context for programs

request_types.originating_component: public/js/modules/tabs-manager.js trackTabChange → core.sendAnalyticsData (type='tab'); also public/integration.js exposes window.trackTabChange (separate, sends DIRECTLY to /analytics/supplement, not via core).
request_types.user_action: User clicks a tab.

request_body_fields.name: query
request_body_fields.notes: Note the supplement endpoint uses 'query', not 'originalQuery' — core.sendAnalyticsData renames if the wrong key is provided.

triggering_code.function: window.trackTabChange (independent emitter, never invoked from this repo's modules — appears to be a back-compat exposure)

gaps: tabs-manager.js's trackTabChange originally also included tabId in enrichmentData (core.sendAnalyticsData strips it for tab actions per core-search-manager.js:756-763). FE intent vs server expectation diverged here at some point.

request_types.user_action: User clicks a facet link or facet-clear/breadcrumb link (analytics-manager listens in parallel with facets-manager).

request_body_fields.name: enrichmentData.facetName
request_body_fields.value_or_source: data-facet-name attr OR closest .facet-group .facet-group__title text OR .facet-breadcrumb__category text OR 'unknown'

request_body_fields.name: enrichmentData.facetValue
request_body_fields.value_or_source: data-facet-value attr OR .facet-group__list-link-text OR .facet-breadcrumb__value OR link textContent

request_types.user_action: User clicks an a.pagination__link.

request_body_fields.name: enrichmentData.pageNumber
request_body_fields.notes: Type can be number OR string in same field.

request_types.user_action: User clicks a 'Did you mean' or query-blending suggestion.

request_types.originating_component: public/js/modules/collapse-manager.js trackCollapseEvent / trackShowMoreEvent / trackTabGroupEvent — all → core.sendAnalyticsData with type='tab' (intentionally typed 'tab' for routing purposes, despite being UI interactions)
request_types.user_action: User clicks a facet collapse/expand button, a 'show more' button in a facet group, or the tab-group filter visibility toggle (the dynamically-injected 'Show Filters' / 'Hide Filters' button).
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → proxy /analytics/supplement DIRECTLY (because core's supplement branch is the default for everything that isn't 'click' or 'batch')

request_body_fields.name: enrichmentData.actionType
request_body_fields.notes: DIVERGENCE from supplement endpoint's documented enum (tab|facet|pagination|spelling). core.sendAnalyticsData passes actionType through unchanged for non-canonical values; the proxy may or may not handle 'ui-interaction'.

request_body_fields.name: enrichmentData.elementName
request_body_fields.value_or_source: facet name (sanitized) for facet-group/show-more; absent for tab-group-filters

gaps: actionType='ui-interaction' may not match anything the proxy /analytics/supplement endpoint expects per the proxy dictionary. Worth cross-checking against funnelback-endpoint-dictionary-2026-05-01.json.

request_types.originating_component: public/js/modules/core-search-manager.js fetchClientIp (fallback when SessionService unavailable); also lib/api-client.ts axios request interceptor on client-side requests; also lib/ip-service.ts fetchClientInfo
request_types.user_action: Implicit — fires when a module needs the client IP and SessionService.getSessionIp returns nothing, or when an axios request from inside the browser needs to add IP headers.
request_types.wire_destination: Browser → su-search-dev /api/client-info (LOCAL — does NOT call the proxy)
request_types.proxy_route: n/a — no proxy hop. Documented for completeness because it appears as outbound traffic from FE code.

triggering_code.function: handler — purely inspects req headers and returns IP; no proxy call

request_types.cache_gate: Client-side IP cache in lib/ip-service.ts (5 min) and lib/api-client.ts (1 min). Both stay within the FE app boundary.

cross_cutting_observations.tab_click_assumption_result: CONTRADICTED. Tabs do NOT operate as client-side filters over preloaded all-tabs data. Each tab click (tabs-manager.js handleTabClick → loadTabContent) fires a fresh /proxy/funnelback/search request via core.fetchFromProxy(href, 'search'), bypassing /api/search and its lib/cache.ts layer entirely. The href used is the Funnelback-generated tab anchor's href — Funnelback templates the trinity (collection/profile/form) and tab-scoping params (f.Tabs|...) into that href, and FE forwards them verbatim except for stripping/re-injecting sessionId and clientIp.
cross_cutting_observations.results_response_segmentation: Inconclusive from the FE-only audit. The FE's /api/search response handling treats the body as opaque HTML (innerHTML assignment into '#funnelback-search-container-response'). It does not parse for per-tab metadata or slice into tab-scoped subsets. Combined with the tab-click finding above, the most consistent interpretation is: the initial render returns ONLY the active tab's content (typically Results/all), and each tab activation pulls its own slice. Confirming this requires inspecting an actual response body, which is out of scope.

trinity_universality_vs_variation.universally_fixed_FE_initiated_paths: On the FE-orchestrated paths (/api/search, /api/prefetch, /api/pre-render, /api/suggestions for the general leg), trinity is uniformly collection='seattleu~sp-search', profile='_default', form='partial' (where applicable). The values appear in three places: integration.js config object, search-page-autocomplete.js hardcoded fallbacks (after a window.seattleUConfig optional-chain), and the Next.js API route handler defaults (search.ts, prefetch.ts, pre-render.ts, suggestions.ts). This is the 'three places' duplication CLAUDE.md flagged — the FE-side manifestations of the trinity.
trinity_universality_vs_variation.varies_on_funnelback_response_paths: On the in-page interaction paths (tab-click, facet-select, facet-clear, pagination-click, spelling-suggestion-click), the trinity is whatever Funnelback embedded into the href in the prior response. The FE has no static knowledge of these values — it just forwards href.split('?')[1] verbatim. So profile may be '_default' or something else entirely (e.g., a tab-specific profile if Funnelback's templating produced one); collection may differ; form may or may not appear.
trinity_universality_vs_variation.implication: Trinity discipline on the FE is split: outbound calls the FE constructs from scratch are uniformly canonical; outbound calls the FE forwards from server-supplied hrefs inherit whatever the proxy/Funnelback chose. Any rebuild design that wants to centralize trinity needs to handle both modes.

fe_intent_vs_proxy_reconciliation_diverg: form parameter on /funnelback/search prefetch leg: prefetch.ts does NOT include form='partial' in its backend call params (prefetch.ts:121-130), unlike pre-render.ts and search.ts. If the proxy normalizes /funnelback/search calls to require form='partial', the prefetched cache key may not match a subsequent canonical search request's cache key. Worth verifying against the proxy dictionary.

form parameter on search-page-autocomplete-suggestion-click: the FE-→-/api/search call from search-page-autocomplete.js performSearch does not send form, relying on /api/search's server-side default. Functionally identical wire output, but the FE intent here is implicit rather than explicit.

actionType='ui-interaction' on /analytics/supplement (collapse-manager): not in the documented enum the supplement endpoint advertised (tab|facet|pagination|spelling). Either the proxy accepts arbitrary actionType strings or these events are silently dropped; FE code alone cannot tell.

/analytics/clicks-batch: configured in core.config.analyticsEndpoints.batch and supported in core.sendAnalyticsData's batch branch (core-search-manager.js:673-709), but no FE caller produces type='batch' analytics anywhere in the inspected files. Endpoint is unreachable from the live FE.
/analytics/ (session): configured in core.config.analyticsEndpoints.session but never referenced by sendAnalyticsData or anywhere else. Dead config entry.

tab_id_enumeration_for_supplement_endpoi.extraction_method: tabs-manager.extractCleanTabName → first .tab-name/.tab-title/.tab-label child OR first text node child OR full textContent, then sanitizeTabName strips '(N)' / '[N]' counter patterns, HTML tags, and normalizes whitespace.
tab_id_enumeration_for_supplement_endpoi.notes: These are user-facing display labels, not the URL-safe identifiers (Faculty_Staff/Programs/Results/News) that lib/utils.ts:normalizeTabId produces server-side from /api/search hrefs. Two different tab-naming spaces coexist: display-name (analytics) vs id (cache keys).

tools_path_enumeration.result: EMPTY. core-search-manager.js fetchFromProxy supports type='tools' (case at lines 450-473) which would forward to /proxy/funnelback/tools with a 'path' parameter extracted from url.split('/s/')[1]. However, no FE caller in the inspected files ever invokes fetchFromProxy with type='tools'. The /funnelback/tools endpoint receives no traffic from this FE.
tools_path_enumeration.implication: The proxy audit's gap on opaque 'path' values for /funnelback/tools cannot be filled from FE code — there are no FE-originated 'path' values to enumerate. Either the endpoint is dead, or it is exercised only by a sibling app (su-search A-side, or another integration).
tools_path_enumeration.gap_for_chat: If the proxy logs show /funnelback/tools traffic, it is not coming from this codebase.

integration_js_config_object_drives: header-form-prerender-trigger (only via window.seattleUConfig if overridden — body itself does not include collection/profile)
configuration_source_distribution_across.data_star_attributes_on_input: NONE. No FE call site reads data-collection / data-profile / data-form from the input element. This row in the audit's configuration model is empty for this codebase.

cross_cutting_observations.session_id_propagation_summary: Three independent SessionService consumers: (1) integration.js reads window.SessionService.getSessionId() inline at each call site; (2) search-page-autocomplete.js wraps it in a module-level SessionManager; (3) core-search-manager.js caches it on this.sessionId and refreshes via getSessionId(). All three hit window.SessionService.getSessionId() under the hood, so they agree, but there's no single source-of-truth wrapper.
cross_cutting_observations.client_ip_propagation_summary: Two layers: (a) FE-direct browser→proxy calls sometimes append clientIp as a query param (core.fetchFromProxy when config.enableIpTracking=true) or as a body field (core.sendAnalyticsData supplement/click branches); (b) Next.js API routes resolve via lib/ip-service.getClientInfo(req.headers) and lib/api-client.ts adds X-Forwarded-For + X-Client-IP headers on the backend leg. The /api/client-info endpoint is the canonical resolver consumed by client-side fetchers.

cache_gates_observed.fe_side_pre_request_check: Only one path: pre-render-content-check fires /api/search with X-Requested-With=XMLHttpRequest and inspects X-Cache-Status to decide whether to display pre-rendered content or fall through. This is NOT a cache-only check (it always returns the response body); it just probes via the same /api/search endpoint that does the full call.
cache_gates_observed./api/check-cache.ts: Exists in pages/api/ but no FE module references it. It would be invoked as /api/check-cache?query=...&collection=...&profile=...&. This is a true cache-only probe (returns 404 on MISS, 200 with TTL on HIT). Endpoint is reachable but unused by inspected FE code.
cache_gates_observed.implication: /api/check-cache appears to be vestigial from a previous FE design. The current FE uses /api/search itself as the probe.

cross_cutting_observations.search_bundle_js_inertness_confirmed: public/search-bundle.js contains the same proxy-call patterns (proxyBaseUrl, fetchSuggestions calling /api/suggestions, performSearch calling /api/search, sendBeacon to /analytics/click and /analytics/supplement) that are live in integration.js + search-page-autocomplete.js. CLAUDE.md notes it is preloaded by integration.js but not executed. Confirmed not listed as a live request surface in this audit.

global_gaps: Whether /proxy/analytics/supplement accepts actionType='ui-interaction' (collapse-manager) — FE code alone cannot answer; cross-check against the proxy dictionary's supplement endpoint enum.

Whether the proxy treats /funnelback/search calls without 'form' (prefetch path) the same as 'form=partial' (search-page/pre-render paths). If they diverge in cache-key generation, prefetched results would not satisfy a subsequent canonical search request.
Whether /proxy/funnelback/tools is exercised at all in the live system — no caller in this FE codebase produces a 'tools' request. Could be entirely served by the A-side FE (su-search) or unused.
Whether /proxy/analytics/clicks-batch and /proxy/analytics/ (session) are exercised — the FE configures both endpoints but produces no traffic to them.

Whether the response body returned by /api/search on initial render contains all-tabs data (and the per-tab calls observed are redundant fetches) or only the active tab's data (and per-tab calls are required for the others). Resolving this requires inspecting actual response bodies, which is out of scope for FE-only audit.

CLAUDE.md states search-page-autocomplete.js lives at public/js/search-page-autocomplete.js, but the actual file is at public/search-page-autocomplete.js. Referenced behavior matches; only the path note in CLAUDE.md is stale.
